A 3-digit palindrome in base \(b\) can be expressed as \(xyx\), where \(x\) and \(y\) are digits in base \(b\). This means the number in decimal is \(x \cdot b^2 + y \cdot b + x = x(b^2 + 1) + y \cdot b\). So, 17 (10001 in base 2) is 21 in base 3, which is a 3-digit palindrome (111) in base 3 (3^2 = 9, 1*9 + 1*3 + 1 = 17). Thus, the number 17 (10001 in base 2) fits our condition.
